free_text: Measurement  of  the   Tensor   Analyzing   Powers  in
Deuteron Photodisintegration.
Inst. Yad. Fiz., Sib. A.N., Novosibirsk
St.Petersburg + Gatchina Inst. Nucl. Phys.
Rutgers University, New Brunswick, NJ
Argonne National Laboratory, Argonne, IL
Tomskiy Politekhnicheskiy Inst., Tomsk
Nat'l  Inst.  for  Nucl.  and  High  Energy
Physics
2 GeV Electron storage ring VEPP-3.
A thin-wall  open-ended storage cell fed by polarized
deuterium gas from Atomic Beam Source (ABS) was used
as an internal target.
Polarised deuterium gas jet with an intensity  of  up
to 8 x 16**16 atoms/s and very high degree of tensor
polarization - above 98 % from extreme values (+1 or
-2), while  vector  polarization was always close to
zero.
The  particle detector consits of two pairs of
arms  for  detecting   protons   and   neutrons   in
coincidence.
Each proton  arm  includes wire chambers for tracking
and 3 layers of plastic scintillators (2 + 12  +  12
cm thick).
Neutron arms consist of plastic  scintiilators:  2-cm
charged particle veto counters followed by 20 cv- 0r
12 + 12 cm scintillator bars.
To  separate  neutrons  and  protons   in
coincidence.
Statistical uncertainties.
Scattering of  an  electron at 0-degree is equivalent
to the radiation of a real  photon,  which  is  then
absorbed by   a   deuteron   -   that   is  why  the
photodisintegration is studied.
Data from Figs. 3 - 5.
Data were compiled  at  Russia  MSU  SINP
CDFE by V.Varlamov.
The main source of  systematic  uncertainty
is the uncertainty in the target polarisation.
The main source of  systematic  uncertainty
is the uncertainty in the target polarisation.
